action.reasoning: Observation: eval failed again after ~3 minutes with exit -9, and stderr shows predict.py is still instantiating a HuggingFace SegFormerForSemanticSegmentation from nvidia/mit-b5, causing missing decode_head keys and likely excessive memory/time. Analysis: the root cause is not TTA itself but nondeterministic checkpoint loading/architecture reconstruction in predict.py/model.py. We already verified best.pt can load cleanly with the HF-style custom model (missing_n=0, unexpected_n=0), so the safest move is to remove all legacy/auto-detection ambiguity and force loading the exact custom architecture used by best.pt. To stay within the remaining budget, use single-scale + optional flip disabled by default for eval throughput, smoke-test on a tiny subset, then resubmit immediately. Decision: rewrite model.py and predict.py into a minimal deterministic HF-backbone custom decoder loader matching best.pt keys exactly, disable expensive default TTA, run a 2-image smoke test, and submit best.pt.
